REQUEST FOR EXCLUSION OF REDUNDANT AND IRRELEVANT DOCUMENTS IN PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE DESIGNATING RECORD ON APPEAL FILED BY DEFENDANT NEWHOUSE, JONATHAN
NOTICE TO REPORTER TO PREPARE TRANSCRIPT (THOMPSON & TORREANO)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT FINDS 26.8 HOURS OF ATTORNEY'S HOURS TO BE REASONABLE AND NECESSARY. ATTORNEY'S BILLABLE RATE OF $275.00 PER HOUR IS REASONABLE. DEFENDANT SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, ROBERT L. FERRIS, ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $7,370.00. INTEREST SHALL ACCRUE ON THIS AWARD FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER AT THE LEGAL RATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Feb-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The Court finds 26.8 hours of attorney's hours to be reasonable and necessary. Attorney's billable rate of $275.00 per hour is reasonable. Defendant shall pay plaintiff's counsel, Robert L. Ferris, attorney's fees in the amount of $7,370.00. Interest shall accrue on this award from the date of this order at the legal rate. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
